fundamentals.name: Datadog, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Datadog, Inc. operates an observability and security platform for cloud applications in the United States and internationally. The company's products comprise infrastructure and application performance monitoring, log management, observability pipelines, synthetics, real user monitoring, product analytics, continuous profiler, database monitoring, data observability, LLM observability, error tracking, network monitoring, incident response, workflow automation and App builder, event management, bits AI SRE, cloud cost management, cloud security, code security, cloud SIEM, threat management, sensitive data scanner, and CI visibility. Datadog, Inc. was incorporated in 2010 and is headquartered in New York, New York.

judgment.summary: High-growth company with strong revenue and earnings expansion, but carries a premium valuation and thin operating margins.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 18.3%; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 32.2% YoY and quarterly earnings growth 113.4% indicate strong business expansion.
Gross margin of 79.9% is best-in-class, supporting high revenue growth.
Forward P/E of 84.04 is lower than trailing P/E of 629.46, suggesting expected earnings growth.
Free cash flow of $936.73M indicates healthy cash generation.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 629.46 and Price/Sales of 23.19 suggest a very high valuation.
Operating margin of 0.8% and profit margin of 3.7% are thin despite strong revenue growth.
Return on assets of -0.2% indicates inefficiency in asset utilization.
Debt/Equity ratio of 32.22 is high, though current and quick ratios are strong.
Mean analyst target of $224.68 is below the current spot price.

news.paragraph: Datadog's stock is experiencing upward momentum, driven by positive analyst sentiment and recent strong performance metrics. JPMorgan maintains a bullish outlook, seeing significant upside potential, while Wedbush is also strongly recommending the stock. Despite some analysts like Goldman Sachs maintaining a 'Sell' rating, the overall narrative is bolstered by Datadog achieving its first $1 billion quarter and raising its 2026 outlook, alongside impressive earnings growth.
